title: ceropteris ferruginea
intro: Ceropteris ferruginea, also known as the rusty club fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Cyatheaceae. It is native to tropical Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and supporting digestion, making it a popular herbal remedy. In terms of herbal preparations, ceropteris ferruginea is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
